notes: Evolved to SAP ERP then SAP ECC then SAP S/4HANA; R/3 mainstream maintenance ended ~2027
Aberdeen's recommended platform; 45%+ RISC/Unix market share 1992; PA-RISC architecture through 7 generations; Oracle's primary Unix revenue platform
5th DEC Unix attempt in 5 years; Aberdeen found ISVs still considered it beta; no TPC-A results available at June 1993; DEC itself told Aberdeen OpenVMS was primary OS
15% RISC/Unix market share 1992; AIX 3.2 stability issues; RIOS processor end-of-life visible; successor PowerPC 600 not yet released
Stability reported poor; IBM created Toronto development center to improve; AIX/6000 successor expected before end 1993; AIX still active as AIX 7.x on IBM Power Systems
Top-tier Unix stability; HP led industry with OpenView extensions; HP-UX 11i still supported until end 2025
SAP R3 back-end database; Oracle uses HP 9000 as Unix reference port; Oracle SQL*NET for distributed connectivity
7 generations of PA-RISC by 1993; HP-UX TPC-A results highest in industry; succeeded by Intel Itanium in HP-UX servers
IBM's planned successor to RIOS; part of AIM alliance; not yet in production for multiuser systems in June 1993
DEC's stated primary OS for multiuser production computing (not OSF1); still maintained by VMS Software Inc.
HP 890-400 TPC-A results higher than IBM 3090-200; DEC Alpha/OSF1 TPC-A results not yet available June 1993
Hercules' existing mainframe to be replaced; IBM will not run TPC-A tests on 3090; target for mainframe elimination by Q1 1996
